data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 8937 "Producer<->consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding.

File-disjointness: 8937 file_impact = _snapshot_schema.py (new), tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_snapshot_contract.py (new), fetcher.py (producer validates vs schema). Disjoint from every sibling's edits — fe57 (tests/fixtures/jira/, _fakes.py, capture script), 59ef (test_acliclient_contract.py, acli_graph.py), 33d0 (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), 2359 (batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py), 822a (e2e_field_validation_probe.sh, test_roundtrip.py). No sibling co-edits fetcher.py or the two new files; fetcher.py is only READ by fe57's fake through fetch_snapshot.

7-mode walk:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. No sibling touches 8937's three files; the schema module is net-new and fetcher.py is consumed (read), not co-edited.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. 8937's schema pins comment(nested)/issuelinks/parent/scalars; fe57 AC2 serves fixtures through fetch_snapshot with NO shape transformation, feeding exactly the shapes 8937's schema targets — mutually reinforcing. The labels list-vs-count representation conflict tracked on 2359↔33d0 is outbound-telemetry/applier-internal; 8937's read-side snapshot-entry schema neither asserts nor depends on labels_applied representation, so it is untouched.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 8937 correctly declares depends_on fe57 (round-trip needs the fixtures/fake). Fixtures (→fe57), applier collapse (→33d0), telemetry (→2359) are explicitly scoped OUT, consistent with the roster; no missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. Distinct surfaces/files: 8937 = assembled snapshot-entry SCHEMA + producer→consumer key/shape round-trip (test_snapshot_contract.py); 59ef = enrichment-METHOD return-shape fidelity (test_acliclient_contract.py); 33d0 = outbound-only APPLICATION correctness; 822a = BIDIRECTIONAL inbound↔outbound behavioral ROUND-TRIP (test_roundtrip.py). Complementary, not duplicative; 8937 declares the others OUT consistently.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. 8937's round-trip targets "the production path" abstractly and includes …→applier; 33d0's end-state IS the single production applier, so 8937's test binds to the survivor by construction (8937 does not edit the applier files — no same-region co-edit with the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier sequencing cluster). The only hard ordering edge (fixtures before the consuming test) is captured by depends_on fe57.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The new _snapshot_schema.py SSOT that fetcher.py validates against is additive; 8937 AC4 asserts semantic keys/types/round-trip (no whole-blob golden equality, no mock-call-count), consistent with the epic's anti-change-detector posture / "no net increase in brittle tests." No sibling forced into a conflicting conformance.
- Residual references — BENIGN. Every schema field references a live producer/consumer surface. 33d0 deletes the DEAD typed-leaf applier, but 8937 exercises the PRODUCTION path, so 33d0's deletion strands nothing 8937 references.

NOTE on the anti-change-detector guardrail: per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause and 8937's own description ("Owns the epic's guardrail artifact") + 8937 AC5, this artifact is OWNED BY THIS CHILD (story B = 8937). It is therefore neither a roster coverage gap nor a G4 cross-child inconsistency — it is an in-scope deliverable of 8937 itself.

VERDICT (parent × 8937): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.
